mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy and reduced earnings. This mechanism is active when inflation exceeds a critical threshold.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth consistently exceeds 3.5% over a 3-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, indicated by PAYEMS, reflects a robust economy with strong consumer demand and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This is an always-on trend signal, reflecting underlying economic health.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and SPX 60-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations are revised downwards. This mechanism activates when the decline becomes substantial.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods when INDPRO 3-month annualized growth falls below -2.0%, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth, as measured by RSAFS, indicates healthy consumer spending and confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon. This is a continuous indicator of consumer strength.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward returns of SPX show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over a 5-year period, as measured by a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate, combined with a falling federal funds rate, signals a recessionary environment where the Fed is actively easing, but the underlying economic weakness still drives negative S&P 500 forward returns due to earnings pressure and uncertainty.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS falls > 0.25pp in 3 months
regime_hint: recessionary environment with active monetary easing
failure_condition: During periods meeting the combined trigger conditions, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-stat < -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates a slowdown in construction and broader economic activity, which negatively impacts related industries and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns as growth expectations diminish. This signal triggers below a critical activity level.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average falls below 1000 (thousands of units)
failure_condition: When HOUST 3-month moving average is below 1000, SPX 20-day returns are positive on average, or the signal's predictive power (e.g., Rank IC) is not statistically significant (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth signals a healthy and expanding economy, which generally fosters investor confidence and corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This is a fundamental, always-on indicator of economic vitality.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth over any 5-year rolling window, with a Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of stable or slightly declining federal funds rates, the cost of capital remains manageable for corporations, supporting valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially after a period of tightening as market uncertainty subsides.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS change over 3 months is between -0.25% and 0.25%
regime_hint: monetary policy pause or mild easing
failure_condition: In periods of FEDFUNDS stability, SPX 20-day returns are negative on average, or the signal's alpha is not statistically significant (t-stat < 1.5) over a 3-year backtest.
